family_name: Prime labelings of zero-divisor graph families
strongest_honest_claim: The zero-divisor campaign now has paired verified p = 11 feeders on both active lines, plus Lean-backed abstract support-template reductions. The strongest honest slice is that prime labelability of Γ(Z_p × Z_25) and Γ(Z_p × Z_p × Z_2) reduces to explicit classwise coprimality partitions on tiny support graphs, and both reductions now survive the campaign-designated p = 11 checkpoint. What remains is to turn those reductions into cleaner family-level support-decomposition lemmas and decide how far the arithmetic program extends at p = 13.
paper_title_hint: Support-template reductions and first arithmetic obstructions in two zero-divisor prime-labeling families
theorem_slice_target: Paired support-template reduction theorems for Γ(Z_p × Z_25) and Γ(Z_p × Z_p × Z_2), with the second theorem stated on the actual support graph A-B, A-C, A-F, B-C, B-E, C-D and reduced to three parameter-sensitive interfaces after setting C = 1.
primary_theorem_slice_statement: For odd prime p, prime labelability of Γ(Z_p × Z_25) and Γ(Z_p × Z_p × Z_2) reduces to explicit classwise interval partitions on tiny support graphs; on the second family, the actual support graph is A-B, A-C, A-F, B-C, B-E, C-D, and after setting the singleton class C to label 1 only A-B, A-F, and B-E remain parameter-sensitive.
fallback_theorem_slice_statement: If the paired theorem slice still does not close, preserve the first honest p = 13 template break as a theorem about the current arithmetic program: either on F25(13) for the four-class line or on the naive {2,3}-smooth template at F2(13).
parameter_sensitive_steps: Supply p - 1 C-labels for Γ(Z_p × Z_25) while keeping enough room inside the first 5p + 19 labels for the 24 labels reserved for A ∪ B.
Choose a four-label sparse barrier set for the B clique in Γ(Z_p × Z_25) whose nontrivial multiples spill into at most the 20 A-slots.
Count enough smooth labels for A ∪ E in Γ(Z_p × Z_p × Z_2) while keeping B as a disjoint large-prime block; the naive {2,3}-smooth template is now the first zero-slack p = 13 case to audit.
Keep the F block away from the prime support assigned to A in Γ(Z_p × Z_p × Z_2).
Show the leftover D block remains unconstrained once the required interfaces are satisfied.
fallback_target: If the arithmetic corollaries do not close, preserve a template-obstruction theorem at p = 13: the unchanged F25 upper-half-prime C program already fails there, and the naive F2 {2,3}-smooth program becomes the first zero-slack test on the parallel line.
next_blocker: Package the paired verified p = 11 feeders into one clean family writeup, extend the Lean family surface from abstract support templates toward reusable support-decomposition lemmas, and then pressure-test the p = 13 frontier starting with Γ(Z_13 × Z_25).
next_feeder_why_decisive: With both p = 11 family lines now verified, Γ(Z_13 × Z_25) is the next user-priority frontier and the first full p = 13 arithmetic stress test on the four-class side; whether it survives cleanly determines how much honest prime-range language the campaign can use.
